description_long: This is a pair of wire strippers or electrician's scissors featuring two elongated, slightly curved handles coated in a yellow, grippy material for comfort and slip resistance. The handles are symmetrical and ergonomically shaped, tapering gently toward the ends. The cutting head is made of black metal and includes a visible pivot joint, with the jaws featuring notched sections for stripping insulation from wires rather than traditional scissor blades. The tool is compact and robust, designed for precise manual tasks such as cutting or stripping wires in urban maintenance or repair settings. There are no visible brand markings or logos on the tool, and the overall finish is matte with a utilitarian appearance.
description: A compact pair of yellow-handled wire strippers with a black metal head and ergonomic, grippy handles. The jaws are notched for wire stripping rather than cutting.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the yellow handles and the black metal head with notched jaws.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the open handles and the notched cutting head facing forward.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the handles and the profile of the notched jaws.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the open handles and the back of the pivot joint.
attribute_handle_type: Symmetrical, ergonomic, ambidextrous handles with yellow plastic coating for enhanced grip.
attribute_blade_joint_type: Visible rivet joint, non-adjustable, standard fixed pivot.
attribute_finger_loop_design: No traditional finger loops; handles are straight and contoured for a full-hand grip, suitable for multiple finger placement.
attribute_blade_tip_style: Notched, blunt tips designed for wire stripping and cutting, not for precision or pointed cutting.
